beats.activity: Wake up, make black coffee, read the Seattle Times at the kitchen table.
beats.reasoning: Mike wakes early by habit—old police discipline. Starts the day with routine: coffee and newspaper, no deviation. Full energy on day one.

beats.activity: Morning walk around Wallingford neighborhood, observing street activity and storefronts.
beats.reasoning: Methodical observer by nature; a solo morning walk keeps him sharp and alert. Fits his discipline and solitary preference.

beats.activity: Drive from Wallingford to Downtown Seattle office.
beats.reasoning: Commute to workplace. Light Rail closure doesn't affect him—he drives. Allows time for thinking during the drive, which he values.

beats.activity: Review client files, conduct phone interviews, and prepare security assessments at the office.
beats.reasoning: Private investigator work in morning; methodical and focused. No-nonsense professional hours before mid-day break.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby diner for breakfast and coffee.
beats.reasoning: Short walk to diner—fits his preference for simple food and solitude. Timing allows him to settle in before the 11am FIFA match.

beats.activity: Sit at diner counter, have breakfast, watch USA vs Wales FIFA match on the diner TV.
beats.reasoning: Mike watches the 11am match at the right time while eating breakfast. Diner setting matches his no-frills style; quiet observation suits his personality.

beats.activity: Drive from Downtown to Wallingford to pick up granddaughter from school.
beats.reasoning: Fiercely loyal to family. Afternoon is reserved for granddaughter—this is non-negotiable routine. Drive allows thinking time.

beats.activity: Sit on park bench watching granddaughter play at Woodland Park, occasionally chatting with her.
beats.reasoning: Core part of Mike's week—afternoon at the park with granddaughter. Quiet presence, loyal, observant. Sunny weather makes this perfect.

beats.activity: Drive granddaughter home and return to Wallingford.
beats.reasoning: Short drive back. Granddaughter's parent picks up; Mike heads home.

beats.activity: Long solo drive through Seattle and surrounding areas—thinking, observing, processing the day.
beats.reasoning: Mike takes solitary drives to think. Methodical, disciplined mind needs this decompression. No-nonsense but reflective.

beats.activity: Stop at local diner for simple dinner—burger, coffee, read a bit more.
beats.reasoning: Prefers diners over fancy restaurants. Solo meal, quiet evening routine. Consistent with his no-frills lifestyle.

beats.activity: Return home, watch the evening news, review case notes, read a mystery novel.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down at home before bed. Quiet evening—reading, news, case review. Disciplined routine winding down for sleep.
7 hours 15 minutes of sleep—consistent with early-riser discipline. Ends before 06:00, allowing full overnight span visible on map.

diary: Started the day with black coffee and the Times at the kitchen table—old habits die hard. The morning walk through Wallingford felt good, the neighborhood quiet and familiar. Office work was steady; couple of client calls, nothing urgent. Grabbed breakfast at Matt's around nine-thirty and caught some of the USA-Wales match on their TV. Decent way to kill an hour before picking up my granddaughter from school. We hit Woodland Park, and I watched her run around while I sat on a bench, just taking in the day. After dropping her off, I took a long drive through the city—no real destination, just needed to think. Stopped at Beth's for a burger and coffee before heading home. Evening was quiet: news, case notes, and a mystery novel. The city felt right today, sunny and unhurried.
thought_process: The light rail closure didn't really affect me since I drive anyway, and honestly, having the granddaughter to pick up gave the day structure. I found myself drawn to the diner at Matt's partly because of the match—old cop in me still likes having something on in the background, something to observe. The long drive after lunch was deliberate; I needed space to process. At sixty-eight, I'm learning that retirement isn't about doing nothing; it's about choosing what matters. The granddaughter, the cases, the quiet mornings—those matter. The World Cup, the maintenance schedules, the city's rhythms—they all just flow around me now. I felt calm today, content even. That's a shift from my police days.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
